strengths: Derives algorithms from state transitions instead of memorizing solutions
Challenges mentor assumptions and validates reasoning with counterexamples
gaps: Initially optimizes implementation before proving correctness
Should separate correctness proofs from efficiency arguments
Initially performs exhaustive case analysis before identifying the underlying state abstraction
common_failures: May change initialization before questioning loop boundaries
Tends to reason with sign combinations before identifying the minimum state required

history.notes: Derived Kadane's algorithm from first principles. Correct Java implementation without algorithm reveal.

Derived Maximum Product Subarray from first principles. Identified the need to preserve both running maximum and running minimum products. Derived three valid transitions (start new, extend previous max, extend previous min) and implemented an O(n)/O(1) Java solution independently.
weaknesses_detected: Started with exhaustive sign-case analysis instead of searching for a higher-level abstraction.
Initially focused on implementation before identifying the minimal state.
lessons_learned: Identify the minimum state that must survive to the next iteration.
Think in terms of valid state transitions instead of enumerating cases.
Preserve previous state before updating dependent variables.
A previous minimum can become the next maximum after multiplying by a negative.
